finding: Multiple AI captains can hold parallel-safe Broker lanes, but the shared Git index has no owner/lease model. One agent can see another active task's staged files, manually unstage or restore them to make its own commit pass, or be blocked by foreign staged/untracked governance bundles even when Broker says the source scopes are parallel-safe.
expectedBehavior: ATM should treat the Git index and destructive worktree/index Git commands as governed shared resources: foreign active staged files must be protected from unstage/restage/restore/reset/checkout/clean by other agents unless a highest-authority explicit keyword/lease is supplied, and Broker should expose an index lane/queue so agents can commit in order without hand-editing each other's staged state.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced on 2026-07-12 while TASK-AAO-0188 attempted to commit a parallel-safe fixture isolation fix while TASK-AAO-FABLE-003 was active. Fable's untracked `.atm/history/evidence/TASK-AAO-FABLE-003.bundle-manifest.json` blocked `node atm.mjs git commit` with `ATM_HOOK_GENERATED_RESIDUE_BLOCKED`; a subsequent operator path suggested unstaging foreign `taskflow.ts`, exposing that agents can mutate another actor's staged index state outside Broker arbitration. User then clarified that `git restore` and similarly severe Git commands must also be prohibited unless a highest-specific authorization keyword is present. Additional live repro while closing TASK-AAO-0188: `taskflow pre-close` recommended `git restore --staged` against foreign staged `packages/cli/src/commands/hook/pre-commit.ts`; `git commit --defer-foreign-staged --dry-run` accepted foreign FABLE bundle manifests as deferred warnings, but the real pre-commit hook re-blocked the same residue because wrapper defer semantics are not transmitted to the hook; `tasks release` left nested `taskDirectionLock.status: active` in released locks, so Broker still reported active-task-scope conflicts; an emergency gitHookBypass lease was consumed by a failed Broker-conflict preflight before any commit was created; and the recovery hint pointed to `backend.brokerConflictOverride`, which would be too dangerous without a paper resolution artifact because it could bypass protection for another agent's staged file. Follow-up repro while trying to safely finish FABLE-005: `next --claim` ignored a freshly written `atm.brokerConflictResolution.v1` artifact (`BCR-9ybygk`) and still reported missing resolution; `--no-verify --dry-run` only surfaced `ATM_EMERGENCY_LANE_APPROVAL_REQUIRED`, but the real commit then surfaced `ATM_GIT_COMMIT_BROKER_CONFLICT_OVERRIDE_REQUIRED` for TASK-AAO-0188/TASK-GIT-0013, so dry-run did not faithfully predict the write gate and can consume an emergency lease on a commit that never reaches Git. 2026-07-13: `TASK-AAO-0189` was formalized as `TASK-GIT-0015` and closed with delivery `329890f7`, close `f8e7a6f`, and planning mirror `cfb724de`. The implementation adds shared git-index ownership evidence, `ATM_INDEX_FOREIGN_ACTIVE_STAGED`, `indexLane`, ATM stage/destructive override lease creation, removes raw `git restore --staged` and dirty-file `git restore --` remediation from core taskflow/commit paths, and keeps TASK-AAO-0188 done/no-residue with `node --strip-types tests/cli/task-ledger-fixture-isolation.test.ts` passing.
followUp: Keep follow-up rows 168-170 for remaining UX/performance refinements; core P0 staging-index arbitration is closed by TASK-GIT-0015.
